basicDetails.schemeName: Haryana Post Matric Scholarship For Minority

schemeSubCategory.value: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend
schemeSubCategory.label: Education and training grants, fellowship, stipend

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md:
Launched in June 2006 under the Prime Minister's New 15 Point Programme for the Welfare of Minorities, the "Post-Matric Scholarship for Students Belonging to the Minority Communities" by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India aims to provide better opportunities for higher education to meritorious students from minority communities. 
Through this scheme, scholarships including admission fees, tuition fees, and maintenance allowance ranging from ₹230 to ₹1,200 per month are provided to students from Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, and Zoroastrian (Parsis) communities whose family income is less than ₹2,00,000 per annum. The State Government of Haryana implements the scheme in collaboration with the Union Ministry of Minority Affairs.
schemeContent.benefits_md: **1.** **Admission and Tuition Fee:** - Classes 11 and 12: Up to ₹7,000/- per annum - Technical/Vocational Courses (11 and 12): Up to ₹10,000/- per annum - Undergraduate/Postgraduate: Up to ₹3,000/- per annum **2.** **Monthly Maintenance Allowance (for 10 months):** **1. Classes 11 and 12 (including technical courses):** - Hostellers: ₹380/- per month - Day Scholars: ₹230/- per month **2. Undergraduate/Postgraduate (non-technical):** - Hostellers: ₹570/- per month - Day Scholars: ₹300/- per month **3. M.Phil and Ph.D.:** - Hostellers: ₹1,200/- per month - Day Scholars: ₹550/- per month
schemeContent.exclusions_md:
1. More than two students from the same family.
1. Students availing benefits under any other scholarship scheme.
1. Students failing to maintain 50% marks in previous examinations.
schemeContent.briefDescription: Launched in June 2006 under the Prime Minister's New 15 Point Programme for the Welfare of Minorities, the "Post-Matric Scholarship for Students Belonging to the Minority Communities" by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India, aims to provide better opportunities for higher education.

children.text: Launched in June 2006 under the Prime Minister's New 15 Point Programme for the Welfare of Minorities, the "Post-Matric Scholarship for Students Belonging to the Minority Communities" by the Ministry of Minority Affairs, Government of India aims to provide better opportunities for higher education to meritorious students from minority communities.

Through this scheme, scholarships including admission fees, tuition fees, and maintenance allowance ranging from ₹230 to ₹1,200 per month are provided to students from Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, and Zoroastrian (Parsis) communities whose family income is less than ₹2,00,000 per annum. The State Government of Haryana implements the scheme in collaboration with the Union Ministry of Minority Affairs.
Admission and Tuition Fee:
Classes 11 and 12: Up to ₹7,000/- per annum
Technical/Vocational Courses (11 and 12): Up to ₹10,000/- per annum
Undergraduate/Postgraduate: Up to ₹3,000/- per annum
Monthly Maintenance Allowance (for 10 months):
1. Classes 11 and 12 (including technical courses):
Hostellers: ₹380/- per month
Day Scholars: ₹230/- per month
2. Undergraduate/Postgraduate (non-technical):
Hostellers: ₹570/- per month
Day Scholars: ₹300/- per month
3. M.Phil and Ph.D.:
Hostellers: ₹1,200/- per month
Day Scholars: ₹550/- per month
More than two students from the same family.
Students availing benefits under any other scholarship scheme.
Students failing to maintain 50% marks in previous examinations.
Step 1:
Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents.
Go to
the National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
. and click “Apply Now”.
Guidelines for
Registration
will appear. Scroll to the bottom.
Read carefully the undertaking. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”.
Step 2:
A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory.)
Fill in the details and click “Register”.
Your Application ID and password will be displayed.
The same will also be sent as an SMS to your registered mobile number.
Step 3:
Go to
Login with OTR
Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password.
Type the Captcha and click “Login”.
On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen.
Create a new password and confirm.
Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
Step 4:
On the left pane, click “Application Form." The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill in the details and upload the documents.
You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later.
Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.

applicationProcess.process_md:
**Step 1:** Keep ready the soft copies of the required documents. Go to [the National Scholarship Portal (NSP)](http://www.scholarships.gov.in/). and click “Apply Now”. Guidelines for [Registration](https://scholarships.gov.in/otrapplication/#/registration-page) will appear. Scroll to the bottom. Read carefully the undertaking. Accept the Terms. Click “Continue”. **Step 2:** A Registration Form will appear. (The fields marked as * are compulsory.) Fill in the details and click “Register”. Your Application ID and password will be displayed. The same will also be sent as an SMS to your registered mobile number.
**Step 3:** Go to [Login with OTR](https://scholarships.gov.in/otrapplication/#/login-page). Click on “Login to Apply”. Enter your Application ID and password. Type the Captcha and click “Login”. On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen. Create a new password and confirm. Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
**Step 4:** On the left pane, click “Application Form." The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill in the details and upload the documents. You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later. Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should belong to the Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Zoroastrian (Parsi) community. 1. The applicant should have secured a minimum of 50% marks in the previous final examination. 1. The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/- from all sources. 1. The applicant should be a regular student in a government or private higher secondary school/college/university. 1. The applicant should not be availing of any other scholarship for the same purpose.
> **Preferences:** - Priority to students from BPL families. - 30% of scholarships are earmarked for girl students.

children.text: The applicant should belong to the Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Zoroastrian (Parsi) community.
The applicant should have secured a minimum of 50% marks in the previous final examination.
The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹2,00,000/- from all sources.
The applicant should be a regular student in a government or private higher secondary school/college/university.
The applicant should not be availing of any other scholarship for the same purpose.
Preferences:
Priority to students from BPL families.
30% of scholarships are earmarked for girl students.
